rulings.Case Title: MANDANA FARHANG et al VS. HT OIL KANSAS II LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Parts Of Fourth Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 26, 2011, Line 3, DEFENDANT JOSE ALLEN, SKADDEN ARPS SLATE MEAGHER & FLOM LLP Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Parts Of Fourth Amended Complaint. Denied. The relief sought is not an appropriate use of a motion to strike, which is not designed as a way to eliminate all allegations in a pleading that are not subject to challenge by demurrer, especially in a lengthy pleading like the fourth amended complaint. Moreover, even if the motion to strike procedure was broad enough to afford the relief sought by defendants, because defendants¿ characterization of plaintiff having alleged only three ¿cognizable claims¿ (overbilling, more expensive billing strategy, and protection of investments) is an inaccurate summary of the fourth amended complaint, the motion to strike is based on mistaken premises. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MIGUEL A. PALMA VS. PRUDENTIAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 26, 2011, Line 9, DEFENDANT COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend, if plaintiff is able to do so, to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572) A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant¿s exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GARY WAI SHING WONG et al VS. FIRST CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 26, 2011, Line 10, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., U.S. BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. U.S. Bank¿s demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all of the claims. Wells Fargo¿s demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first, second and third claims and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth claim. The order filed July 21, 2011 gave plaintiffs leave to plead the first three claims as to this defendant with more particularity yet plaintiffs failed to do so. Plaintiffs¿ allegations of agency, ratification, and conspiracy are conclusions of law, not required ultimate facts. The fourth claim is not permitted as to both demurring defendants because plaintiffs did not obtain leave to amend to plead that claim. In addition, Civil Code 2932.5 is inapposite. Plaintiffs are provided one last opportunity to plead further facts showing how Well¿s Fargo¿s conduct, including the request for the $40,859 to be placed in trust, was fraudulent, unfair, or unlawful. The current allegations do not clearly state how the payment was induced by fraud and is unfair and unlawful. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: 100824 CANADA INC., A CANADIAN CORPORATION VS. JOHN HWANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 26, 2011, Line 11, DEFENDANT JOHN HWANG'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. The order signed by judge Goldsmith permits defendant to re-assert his argument on this demurrer that the claims alleged by plaintiff are derivative in nature. Because those claims are based on an alleged misconduct done to the corporation rather than plaintiff alone, they are derivative in nature and must be so plead. Defendant¿s statute of limitations argument lacks merit. rulings.Case Title: JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS VS. POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 26, 2011, Line 15, PLAINTIFF JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS 401(K) PLAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted as to the fourth and fifth causes of action and denied as to the sixth cause of action. Plaintiff satisfied his burden of production on the fourth and fifth causes of action that he is entitled to a judgment in his favor and defendant did not present any evidence creating a triable issue as to those claims. Defendant did not create a triable issue that rule 3-300 invalidates the note or security agreement since there is no evidence that, at the time the note and security agreement were executed, there was an attorney-client relationship between plaintiff and defendant. Nor did defendant present evidence that the lack of consent of other partners invalidates either the note or security agreement. There is no dispute that by January 31, 2011 defendant knew that plaintiff had called the loan and that, as of three months later, defendant had not repaid any portion of the loan. The January 28 letter is sufficient notice of the call of the loan. Thus, even assuming that the October 31 email attachment was not received by defendant and/or is privileged, there is sufficient non-privileged evidence that the loan has been called and not repaid ninety days after being called. (SEE PART 2) = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS VS. POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff James S. Madow, Individually And On Behalf Of His Motion For Summary Adjudication (Part 2)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 26, 2011, Line 15, Plaintiff James S. Madow, Individually And On Behalf Of His 401(K) Plan's Motion For Summary Adjudication (Part 2). As distinct from the fourth and fifth causes of action, which do not depend on a determination of the precise amount owed by defendant to plaintiff, the sixth cause of action as pled by plaintiff seeks a determination that plaintiff may apply the proceeds of the sale of the collateral to the debt owed by defendant. However, since plaintiff has not presented any facts as to the current amount of the debt, plaintiff has not satisfied his burden on production on the sixth cause of action. rulings.Case Title: CENTURY 21 PINNACLE AKA PINNACLE HOMES & ESTATES VS. JOSE L. CAMPOS et al

rulings.Case Title: JOANNE REINHART VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bank Of America, N.A., Individually And As Successor By Merger To Bac Home Loans Servicing, Lp'S Demurrer To Complaint (Part 2)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 26, 2011, Line 20, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO BAC HOME LOANS SERVICING, LP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT (PART 2). The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for quiet title is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff¿s holder in due course argument fails and plaintiff still has title. ( See Ortiz v. Accredited Home Lenders, Inc. (S.D. Cal. 2009) 639 F.Supp.2d 1159, 1168). The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for breach of implied covenant is sustained with leave to amend to allege the existence of a contract between the parties. The demurrers to the seventh cause of action for intentional misrepresentation and eight cause of action for negligent misrepresentation are sustained with leave to amend to plead more facts. While paragraphs.10 and 52-62 have some detail, plaintiff needs to add more facts to comply with Lazar v. Sup. Court. (1996) 12 Cal.App.4th 631, 645. The demurrer to the ninth cause of action for promissory estoppel is overruled because this claim is adequately pled. The demurrer to the eleventh cause of action for violation of CC 1798.82 is sustained with leave to amend to plead more facts. The demurrer to the twelfth cause of action for violation of the UCL is overruled because this claim is adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOANNE REINHART VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 26, 2011, Line 20, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO BAC HOME LOANS SERVICING, LP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer ruling is sustained without leave to amend in part, sustained with ten days leave to amend in part and overruled in part as follows: The demurrer to the first cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend. A party does not need to hold the notice to commence the foreclosure process. (See CC 2924(a)(1); Gomes v. Countrywide (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 1149, 1155). The demurrer to the second cause of action for injunctive relief is sustained without leave to amend. Injunctive relief is a remedy, not an independent cause of action. ( See Marlin v. Aimco Venezi, LLC (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 154, 162). The demurrer to the third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff fails to plead outrageous conduct. The demurrers to the fourth cause of action for negligently infliction of emotional distress and the tenth cause of action for negligence are sustained without leave to amend. Negligent infliction of emotional distress is not an independent claim. (See Ess v. Eskaton Properties, Inc. (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 120, 125). Moreover, ¿a financial institution owes no duty of care to a borrower when the institution's involvement in the loan transaction does not exceed the scope of its conventional role as a mere lender of money.¿ (Nymark v. Heart Fed. Sav. & Loan Ass¿n. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 1089, 1096). (SEE PART 2) = (302/HEK)
